description: THE dominant kernel: func_solve_body_monolith_c???_?_kernel_1_range_for. ~54% of GPU time on the the production benchmark. Per the team analysis (h100_vs_mi300x_kernel_comparison.txt) this single kernel accounts for ~56% of the H100<->MI300X gap. Compiler-layer fix already shipped (AccVGPR + global_load via codegen pass, TICKET/4420). DSL-layer optimization is the next lever and lives in func_solve_iter and its callees, which are all inlined into this monolith kernel by Quadrants.

edit_files.rationale: func_solve_body_monolith itself (~line 3160), func_solve_iter (~3067), func_linesearch_batch (~2432), func_update_constraint_batch (~2625), func_update_gradient_batch (~2731), func_terminate_or_update_descent_batch (~2846), func_solve_init (~2940), and the func_ls_init_*/func_ls_point_fn_* helpers. ALL of these get inlined into the monolith kernel by Quadrants.
func_solve_mass_batch / func_solve_mass_entity (the per-CG-iter Cholesky solve). Called from func_update_gradient_batch and inlined into the monolith kernel.

correctness_pytest_extra_args: -k "solver or constraint or rigid or not (test_convexify or test_mesh_repair or test_mesh_primitive_COM)"
